perturbation.original_text: This Sponsorship Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the 14th day of May, 1999 ("Effective Date"), by and between Intuit Inc. a Delaware corporation, located at 2550 Garcia Ave., Mountain View, California 94043 ("Intuit"), and Stamps.com Inc., a Delaware corporation, located at 2900 31st Street, Suite 150, Santa Monica, CA 90405-3035 ("Client").
perturbation.changed_text: This Sponsorship Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into as of the 14th day of May, 1999 ("Effective Date"), by and between Intuit Inc. a Delaware corporation, located at 2550 Garcia Ave., Mountain View, California 94043 ("Intuit"), and Stamps.com Inc., a Delaware corporation, located at 2900 31st Street, Suite 150, Santa Monica, CA 90405-3035 ("Partner").
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Client' to 'Partner' in the introduction, there is ambiguity about whether 'Client' and 'Partner' are the same entity throughout the rest of the document. This creates uncertainty regarding the obligations and rights applicable to each party, particularly impacting financial responsibilities of each party in Section 10 where the contradition takes place
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. FEES 10.1 Sponsorship, Advertising and Exclusivity Fees. Client will pay Intuit --------------------------------------------- sponsorship and advertising fees of $2,644,010 and an exclusivity fee of $661,003. Such fees shall be paid to Intuit as follows. An initial fee of $[***] shall be due and payable on the Effective Date. The remaining --- balance of $[***] shall be paid to Intuit in 12 equal monthly installments --- of [***]. Each monthly installment should be payable in advance and due no --- later than the fifth (5th) of the month.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Client' to 'Partner' in the initial definition section of the contract directly introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for the 'Sponsorship, Advertising and Exclusivity Fees' outlined in Section 10. By renaming 'Client' to 'Partner', the contract now lacks clarity on whether the financial obligations detailed in Section 10 apply to 'Partner', 'Client', or both. This directly contradicts the original intention and creates uncertainty in the enforcement of the financial terms, thus constituting a strong in-text contradiction based on Misaligned Terminology.

perturbation.original_text: Client wishes to promote its business to users of the Intuit Sites through promotions and advertising in various portions of the Intuit Sites.
perturbation.changed_text: Stamps.com Inc. wishes to promote its business to users of the Intuit Sites through promotions and advertising in various portions of the Intuit Sites.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Client' to 'Stamps.com Inc.' creates an in-text contradiction regarding which entity is subject of the contract. The 'Client' term has been defined as 'Partner' in the Preamble, which leads to in-text contradition on Section 8.1, about Client obligations, now becomes unclear as to whether the Stamps.com Inc or the Client are responsible.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.  LAUNCH DATE, RESPONSIBILITY FOR INTUIT SITES AND REPORTING 8.1 Client Obligations. Client will use reasonable efforts to assist Intuit in ------------------ implementing the promotional placements and advertising described in the Agreement. The parties recognize that the Launch Date can be met only if Client provides final versions of all Client Graphics, text, Banner Advertisements and other promotional media and valid URL links necessary to implement the promotional placements and
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Client' to 'Stamps.com Inc.' directly contradicts the original definition of 'Client' as 'Partner' outlined in the Preamble. This contradiction manifests in Section 8.1, titled 'Client Obligations,' where the responsibilities are assigned to 'Client.' By replacing 'Client' with 'Stamps.com Inc.,' it creates ambiguity and uncertainty as to which entity—the originally defined 'Partner' or 'Stamps.com Inc.'—is responsible for fulfilling the obligations outlined in Section 8.1. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' type because the contract now uses conflicting terms ('Client' as both 'Partner' and 'Stamps.com Inc.') without clear definition, leading to contradictory obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 1.4 "Client Competitor" means any of the entities listed on Exhibit A to this Agreement, as such list may be amended by mutual agreement by the parties, provided such entity derives any of its annual gross revenues from the sale or delivery of electronic postage or postage meters.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 "Intuit Competitor" means any of the entities listed on Exhibit A to this Agreement, as such list may be amended by mutual agreement by the parties, provided such entity derives any of its annual gross revenues from the sale or delivery of electronic postage or postage meters.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Client Competitor' to 'Intuit Competitor' changes the meaning of the entire section. This creates confusion and in-text contradition in Section 9, since the term Client Competitor is being used to refer to a specific meaning, in this case the 'Client' Stamps.com competitor, and Intuit doesn't have anything to do with their competitors, making the terms not aligned and creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. EXCLUSIVITY Throughout the Term Intuit will not place, and will not allow any party acting on its behalf to place, any graphic, link or other form of advertising or media on any page of the Quicken.com Site and/or on any page on the AOL.com Personal Finance Site (other than the Channel Home Page), which markets or promotes any electronic postage product, postage meter and/or service ("Postage Products") offered by a Client Competitor. Notwithstanding the above, Intuit may include editorial content or tools about or from a Client Competitor and include Client Competitors in directory listings.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Client Competitor" which is then used in Section 9 to limit Intuit from advertising competitors of the client (presumably Stamps.com). The changed text redefines "Client Competitor" to "Intuit Competitor." This creates a contradiction because Section 9 now prohibits Intuit from advertising competitors of Intuit, which doesn't align with the purpose of the original agreement to protect the 'Client' (Stamps.com) from competition. The term 'Client Competitor' in Section 9 is now nonsensical, and the intended meaning is lost, resulting in an in-text contradiction because the obligation created in section 9 is now referencing 'Intuit Competitor' which has nothing to do with the exclusivity agreement initially made. This misalignment is therefore a strong contradiction based on the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.
